events.title: Vocal Jazz Ensemble
events.description: The Vocal Jazz Ensemble performs jazz, blues, rock and gospel standards and new arrangements. Mitch Chakour, director and pianist

events.title: Latinx Coffee Hour
events.description: Creating a safe space for students to share their culture, experiences, and make new connections. Come for coffee, snacks, & good conversation!

events.title: SpACE+ Study & Snacks
events.description: Join us in the center for a quiet night to study and de-stress with snacks. The SpACE+ is a student group for ace and aro communities, but all are welcome to attend.

events.title: Wind Ensemble and Symphony Band
events.description: May the Fourth be with you! Includes music from John Williams "Star Wars" soundtrack Matthew Westgate and Lindsay Bronnenkant, conductors

events.title: HR is Here for You
events.description: Looking for job opportunities at UMass Amherst? Need help with your application or have questions about the hiring process? Are you a new employee with questions about onboarding? UMass Amherst Human Resources is here to help! The UMass HR team offers monthly community office hours at UMass Downtown to provide application assistance and interview guidance for prospective employees and CORI verifications and onboarding support for new hires. No appointment necessary. Just stop by during UMass HR's community office hours!

events.title: Adoption Across the Life Course: The Needs and Experiences of Adult Adoptees in the USA and UK
events.description: Adoption across the life course The Rudd Adoption Research Program will be hosting a multi-date webinar series titled Adoption Across the Life Course: The Needs and Experiences of Adult Adoptees in the USA and UK. The webinars will feature five recent research studies from both the US and the UK. These will focus on different aspects of life for adopted people as adults (neurodiversity; becoming a parent; adoption consciousness; mental health; use of social media) as well as messages for the policy and practice. Each session will include adult adoptees speaking about their lived experiences. T

events.title: Against Money: J.W. Mason and Arjun Jayadev
events.description: Free and open to the public Money is often seen as a neutral tool — a mere veil over the ‘real’ economy. “Against Money” challenges that view. Join authors J. W. Mason and Arjun Jayadev, along with panelists, Christine Desan, Gerald Epstein, and Perry Mehrling, as they explore how money shapes the world it claims to measure. The discussion is moderated by Robert Pollin. The event is sponsored by the Political Economy Research Institute and the UMass Amherst Economics Department’s History and Political Economy Workshop. Download flyer

events.title: Therapy Dog Hour at Stonewall Center
events.description: Drop-in any Tuesday that classes are in session from 4:30-5:30 p.m. De-stress with Mini and Bentley. Open to all students.

events.title: BIPOC Critique Group
events.description: BIPOC Critique Group is a supportive, intellectually rigorous space for BIPOC visual artists to share their work and engage in meaningful dialogue. With the insights of ally scholars and community members, we foster cultural, intellectual, and contextual conversations that honor the depth and complexity of BIPOC experiences. Meets two Tuesdays 5pm to 7pm most months. October 21, 2025 November 18, 2025 November 25, 2025 December 9, 2025 January 6, 2026 January 20, 2026 February 3, 2026 February 17, 2026 March 10, 2026 March 24, 2026 April 7, 2026 April 21, 2026 May 5, 2026

events.title: BPQC (Bi/Pan/Queer Collective) BBQ Party
events.description: Food, music, and games! Open to all. The rain location is the Stonewall Center, 403 Goodell Hall.

events.title: The Translated Book Club: Heart Lamp
events.description: The Translated Book Club promotes the reading of literature translated into English and recognizes and supports translators and their publishers. Each meeting is hosted at UMass Downtown by a UMass Amherst faculty member or graduate student and designed for readers of diverse backgrounds with no specific language requirement other than English. Sponsored by the Gentzler Translation Center, the club is free and open to community members from high school students to retirees, with a maximum of 15 readers per meeting. Advanced sign-up is required at https://www.umass.edu/downtown/news-events. The

events.title: Libraries' Page to Screen Club
events.description: Drop in to the Libraries' multimedia book club, where we read a book and then watch the screen adaptation! We meet for several weeks to talk about the book, then watch the screen adaptation together and compare. All sessions are 7-8:30 p.m. except for screenings, which are 7-9:30 p.m. The breakdown for what pages/sections to read will be determined at a later planning session. Little Women, Louisa May Alcott February 3 February 10 February 17 February 24 March 3 - Screening Stories of Your Life and Others, Ted Chiang March 10 March 17 - No meeting; spring vacation March 24 - Screening Jurassic

events.title: All Campus Choir
events.description: The new ensemble, All Campus Choir, performs its end-of-semester concert in Bezanson, free and open to the public. Ben Coakley, conductor

events.title: Rhinoceros
events.description: One pleasant Sunday morning in a quiet provincial French town, a drink between two friends is rudely interrupted by a rhinoceros barreling through. Clownish chaos ensues in Eugene Ionesco’s 1959 Theater of the Absurd masterpiece, Rhinoceros, and indeed, something very absurd happens — people start turning into the titular rhinos. How the villagers deal, or don't deal, with this pachydermal problem makes for comedy and commentary in UMass Theater's upcoming production of Rhinoceros. The show opens on May 1 and runs through May 9 in the Rand Theater. Tickets are on sale now through the Fine Arts

events.title: Warm Sips Wednesdays: Building Community One Cup at a Time
events.description: Would you like some hot beverage to take to class with you or drink on your way to the next class? How about sipping your cup over some meaningful conversations? Stop by WOCLN’s table for a free cup of coffee, tea or hot cocoa every Wednesday this spring, 10-11:30 AM in the Lobby of New Africa House. Wednesdays, 10-11:30 AM, starting Wednesday February 4.Lobby of New Africa House Sponsored by Women of Color Leadership Network (WOCLN).

events.title: Drawing Curious Spaces Exhibit Reception - Mass Aggie Seed Library
events.description: A special showcase and reception of the tile drawings done over the year as part of the Drawing Collections (Fall 2025) and Drawing Curious Spaces (Spring 2026) series. The Drawing Curious Spaces Series is comprised of a number of informal gatherings at some quirky and less know spaces on campus focused on slow seeing and drawing. No registration necessary; drop-in style means you come for what you can. Each gathering is a stand-alone event. No drawing experience necessary; each attendee gets use of a drawing kit. This project is funded by the Office of Equity and Inclusion.

events.title: Transfer Connect: Flower Wreath Making
events.description: Looking for a fun way to unwind and get creative? Join the Undergraduate Student Success Center for Transfer Flower Wreath Making on Wednesday, May 6th, from 4:00 PM to 6:00 PM in Goodell 633! Design and create your own beautiful flower wreath while connecting with fellow transfer students in a relaxed and welcoming environment. All supplies will be provided, just bring your creativity. Come hang out, de-stress, and leave with a handmade piece to brighten your space. Children are welcome! 🌸

events.title: W. E. B. Du Bois Center Fellow Talk: Matheus Gato de Jesus
events.venue: Library, W. E. B. Du Bois
events.description: RSVP here. Livestream link here. The Reception of Du Bois in Brazilian Social Thought This presentation aims to present the context and some of the impasses surrounding the reception of Du Bois in Brazilian social thought, focusing on four aspects: a) the context of affirmative action and quota policies in Brazilian universities and the emergence of new knowledge policies; b) the recent canonization of the author as a founding classic of modern sociology; c) the dialogue between Brazilian Black movements and Du Bois's thought; d) the conflicts surrounding the use of the notion of race in scien

events.title: Migratory Bird Week
events.description: Get ready for World Migratory Bird Day with the Natural History Collections! Stop by Morrill 146 on May 4th - May 8th to learn about migratory bird species and brush up on your identification skills. Enter our contest to win a prize! The exhibit is open Monday-Friday, 9:00 am - 5 pm.
